title: salix × grisescens
intro: Salix × grisescens, also known as Willow Gray or Grey Willow, is a hybrid plant species derived from the Salix alba and Salix fragilis willow trees. The bark and leaves of this plant contain salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Salix × grisescens have been used to treat various health issues, including arthritis, fever, and headaches, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the bark of Salix × grisescens is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which are applied topically to soothe skin irritations and reduce inflammation.
